sensor_interpretation_note: Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; do not trigger alerts on a single reading — use average of 3 consecutive readings taken 10 minutes apart to confirm threshold breach

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; light readings below 900 lux sustained for 3+ days should trigger alert as new leaves will not develop fenestrations

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; moisture readings above 75% for more than 3 averaged readings indicate overwatering risk; avoid reacting to single spike readings

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; humidity readings consistently above 90% averaged over 3+ readings should trigger airflow alert due to high bacterial disease risk

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; most tolerant plant on the list — only trigger alert if averaged readings stay outside warning range for 5+ consecutive readings

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; leaf shape change from arrow to lobed is a maturity sign not a stress indicator — do not use leaf shape as a sensor trigger

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; humidity averaged above 70% combined with averaged light below 18000 lux is a combined fungal disease risk alert — both conditions together are more critical than either alone

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; averaged moisture below 25% in summer months combined with temp above 35°C is a critical combined bud drop alert — requires immediate watering action

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; humidity averaged above 80% with light below 13500 lux is a powdery mildew combined risk alert; moisture above 80% for 3+ averaged readings indicates waterlogging

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; humidity averaged below 35% is the most critical alert for Croton — spider mite risk; light averaged below 9000 lux will cause leaf color fading to green

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; temperature averaged below 6°C is a critical dormancy/leaf drop alert; moisture averaged above 75% for 3+ readings is a root rot risk

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; humidity averaged above 80% with poor airflow is a powdery mildew alert; temp averaged above 37°C for multiple readings will trigger premature bolting alert

Ideal and warning ranges include ±5% moisture / ±2°C temp / ±5% humidity / ±10% light buffer for sensor variance; moisture averaged above 55% is a critical overwatering alert unique to Aloe — do not water again until averaged moisture reading drops below 15%; this is the lowest moisture threshold of all plants in this dataset
